info.title: MOCA API
info.description:
The unified public API of the **Museum of Crypto Art** — collections, artworks (with original-ratio media), 3D exhibition rooms, and the **Art DeCC0s** knowledge base, aggregated behind a single API key.

All endpoints (except the index) require a MOCA API key sent as `X-API-Key: <key>` or `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.

Responses use the Directus-style envelope: `{ "data": …, "meta": … }` on success and `{ "errors": [{ "message", "extensions": { "code" } }] }` on failure.

tags.name: Artworks
tags.description: Artworks across all museum collections, with normalized media

tags.name: DeCC0s
tags.description: The Art DeCC0s knowledge base, aggregated from api.decc0s.com

tags.name: Search
tags.description: Unified search across the whole museum

tags.name: Library
tags.description: The MOCA Library — RAG Q&A and search over the museum's knowledge base (powered by Cortex)

tags.name: Souls
tags.description: Signed agent identity documents (SOUL files) for NFTs, served by Soulweaver — built for ERC-8004 / ERC-8183 / ERC-8257 integrations

tags.name: Presence
tags.description: Ephemeral presence for the Library — public, nothing persisted server-side

tags.name: Guide
tags.description: The agentic museum guide: exhibition context (rooms, architects, artists, works), visitor question suggestions, and in-persona Q&A. Public — these endpoints power anonymous in-world visitors in Hyperfy and require no API key.

get.description: Service information and the list of available endpoints. The only route that works without an API key.
All published top-level museum collections, each with its child collections.

data.name: Genesis Collection
data.title: The Genesis Collection
data.description: The founding artworks of the museum.

get.description: Paged artworks across the museum. Media is normalized server-side: dead URLs are revived, OpenSea's square-cropped CDN variants are swapped for the **original files**, and `ratio` only ever derives from trustworthy dimensions — render `media.url` at `ratio` and you are showing the artwork the way the artist made it.

parameters.name: collection
parameters.description: Comma-separated collection slug(s) to scope the query.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Match against artwork title or artist name.

get.description: The museum's 3D exhibition architecture. Each room ships a GLB model (`model_url`) whose `Slot_001…Slot_NNN` placeholder meshes mark wall positions for hanging artworks — the same convention the museum's own world builder uses.

get.summary: Get a room's baked slot data
get.description:
Baked artwork anchors for the room's builder GLB: per-slot position, frame size, and a facing-resolved orientation (local +Z points at the viewer, +Y upright). Computed offline by probing the room geometry on both sides of every slot plane — clients can hang works on these anchors verbatim, with no normal-flipping heuristics.

**Public** — no API key required, like the guide endpoints: 3D clients (the world builder, Hyperfy spawns, third-party viewers) consume this anonymously.

404.description: Room not found, or no slot data baked for it yet

get.description: The 10,000 Art DeCC0 entities, aggregated live from the DeCC0s knowledge base (api.decc0s.com) with a short server-side cache. List responses return a lightweight field set; use `fields` to choose your own, or fetch `/v1/decc0s/{id}` for the full record.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Full-text search across the knowledge base.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Comma-separated field selection (the heavyweight `agent_profiles` / `moltbot` blobs are list-excluded regardless).

get.description: One Art DeCC0 entity by token id (1–10000). Add `include=profiles` for the full agent persona blobs (`agent_profiles`, `moltbot` — hundreds of KB), and `include=codex` to embed the character's codex lore document.
One query across artworks, collections, and DeCC0s — grouped results, capped per group.

post.description: RAG Q&A over the MOCA knowledge base: hybrid retrieval, optional knowledge-graph context, cited sources. For token-by-token output use the streaming variant.

question.description: The question to ask the Library

collection_id.description: Scope to one Library collection (see GET /v1/library/collections)

use_agentic.description: Deep-research mode: multi-step agent reasoning (slower, more thorough)

conversation_history.description: Prior turns for follow-up questions (last 20 kept)

example.question: What is the significance of the Genesis Collection?

503.description: This surface is not configured on the deployment

post.description: Same request as `/v1/library/ask`, answered as **Server-Sent Events**. Each `data:` line is a JSON object: `{"content": "…"}` token chunks, one `{"sources": [...]}` event, optional `{"thinking": "…"}` / `{"sub_questions": [...]}` (agentic mode), `{"graph_context": {...}}`, and a final `{"done": true}`. Deep-research streams can run for minutes — disable proxy buffering.

question.description: The question to ask the Library

collection_id.description: Scope to one Library collection (see GET /v1/library/collections)

use_agentic.description: Deep-research mode: multi-step agent reasoning (slower, more thorough)

conversation_history.description: Prior turns for follow-up questions (last 20 kept)

503.description: This surface is not configured on the deployment

post.description: Hybrid retrieval (vector + keyword + graph) without answer generation — returns scored excerpts. Cheaper and faster than ask; ideal for building your own UX on top.

503.description: This surface is not configured on the deployment

get.description: The knowledge-base collections available to ask/search against (use their `id` as `collection_id`).

503.description: This surface is not configured on the deployment

get.description: Paginated SOUL files generated for a collection's tokens.

503.description: This surface is not configured on the deployment

get.description: The latest SOUL file for one NFT, including the **EIP-191 verification block** — recover the signer from `verification.message` and compare to `soul.signerAddress` to prove the identity document is authentic. See the [Web3 & agents guide](/web3) for ERC-8004 / ERC-8183 / ERC-8257 integration patterns.

503.description: This surface is not configured on the deployment

get.description: Server-Sent Events feed of Library activity. **Nothing is persisted** — you only receive events broadcast while connected (the docs chat shows this as 'searched since you arrived'). Events: `{"type":"hello","here":n}` on connect, `{"type":"arrived"|"left","here":n}`, and `{"type":"library-search","handle":"…","at":ms}` when someone pings. Questions are never broadcast — only the chosen handle.

post.description: Broadcast the *fact* of a Library search to everyone currently listening — fire it when you ask. Send only a display handle (wallet address, name, or nothing → 'anon'); never the question. Rate-limited per IP.

handle.description: Display handle shown to listeners. Optional.

post.summary: Register an exhibition for the guide
post.description: Register (or refresh) a world-builder exhibition so the museum guide can answer questions about it. Accepts a trimmed `moca-exhibition@1` document — only `id`, `name`, and per-placement room/artwork identity are read; the API enriches architects, descriptions, and artist data from the museum’s own collections. Idempotent per exhibition id. Spawners call this automatically when a curator spawns with a guide — that spawn is the curator’s explicit opt-in to publish the context. Rate-limited per IP. Cortex-generated question starters are added asynchronously after registration.

get.description: The enriched context the guide reasons over: every room with its architect and description, every artwork with its artist — plus distinct artist/architect lists and Cortex question starters.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The exhibition id from the world builder.

get.description: Question starters for a visitor — Cortex-generated starters plus questions templated from the exhibition’s actual works, artists, rooms, and architects. Deterministic per `seed`; vary it to rotate the pool.

parameters.name: seed
parameters.description: Rotation seed (default: derived from the exhibition id).

post.description: Answer a visitor’s question about a registered exhibition. Combines the exhibition context with the museum’s Cortex Library (RAG with citations) and answers in persona — Oblak (DeCC0 #2875) by default, or any Art DeCC0 via `decc0`, a Soulweaver soul via `soulRef`, or a curator-supplied SOUL.md via `soul` (first match wins: soul > soulRef > decc0). Pass the running conversation in `history` so the guide reacts to the dialogue, not just the last question. Falls back to a context-only answer (`fallback: true`) when the Library is unreachable. Rate-limited per IP (~20/min).

history.description: Conversation so far (last ~8 turns are used).

decc0.description: Art DeCC0 token id whose persona the guide adopts. Omit for the default guide.

soul.description: A complete SOUL.md the guide embodies (beats soulRef/decc0).

soulName.description: Display name for the custom soul.

soulRef.description: A Soulweaver soul coordinate — the EIP-191-signed SOUL file is resolved server-side (beats decc0).

session.description: Stable per-visitor id. Enables server-side session memory + the async Library follow-up. Omit for a stateless reply.

speak.description: Synthesize spoken audio for the answer (when the deployment has TTS configured).

voice.description: Venice TTS voice id (e.g. Serena). Empty = the deployment default.

roomUid.description: The placement uid of the room the visitor is in (authoritative spatial signal). Grounds 'what's in this room?' and lets the guide notice when the visitor moves rooms.

visitorPos.description: Fallback spatial signal when roomUid is absent: the visitor's world position (meters); the API maps it to the room they're inside, else the nearest.

focus.description: The single work the visitor is standing in front of — grounds 'which artwork is this?' on the right piece.

example.question: Who designed this room, and what should I look at first?

BearerAuth.description: Your MOCA API key as a bearer token.

Unauthorized.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked API key

RateLimited.description: Rate limit exceeded (default 120 requests/minute per key). Check `Retry-After` and the `X-RateLimit-*` headers.

Upstream.description: The DeCC0s upstream is temporarily unavailable

Media.description: A renderable media variant with a resolved https URL.

url.description: Direct URL (IPFS already resolved to a gateway)

media.description: Best still image — original file preferred over CDN crops

animation.description: Motion variant (video) when the work has one

ratio.description: Trustworthy aspect ratio (width/height); 1 when unknown. Never derived from a square CDN crop.

slots.description: Number of artwork wall slots (source of truth: the smart contract, synced onchain → Directus)

image_url.description: Full-resolution preview image (Directus asset URL, supports ?width=&format= transforms)

thumbnail_url.description: Lightweight preview: a 512px-wide WebP variant of image_url (quality 80), ready to drop into cards or wallet/gallery grids without transforming the multi-MB original yourself.

model_url.description: GLB model download URL — the untouched high-quality version (single-room HQ viewer)

model_optimized_url.description: Optimized GLB download URL (draco-compressed, webp textures) with embedded Slot_NNN placeholder nodes — the variant the exhibition builder uses. For un_MUSEUMs (no authored slots in the model) the slots are generated deterministically from the onchain slot amount. Null until the variant has been generated.

slot_data_url.description: URL of the room's baked slot data (`GET /v1/rooms/{id}/slots`) — per-slot anchors with resolved facing. Null until the data has been baked.

Decc0Summary.description: Lightweight DeCC0 list item. `*_url` fields are resolved asset URLs added by the aggregation layer.

image_ipfs_url.description: ipfs:// URI of the final artwork

allOf.description: Full knowledge-base record (biography, personality, writing style, geography, …). With `include=profiles` it also carries `agent_profiles` and `moltbot`; with `include=codex` it embeds `codex_document`.

codex_document.description: Only with include=codex; null when no codex file exists for this token

Soul.description: A SOUL file: a portable, signed agent identity document for one NFT.

description.description: Artwork description from museum data, clamped.

uid.description: Placement uid from the exhibition export.

architect.description: The room’s architect, from museum data.

GuideContext.description: The enriched context document the museum guide reasons over — the authoritative record of what hangs where in a spawned exhibition.

id.description: Stable exhibition id from the world builder.

starters.description: Cortex-suggested visitor questions (filled in asynchronously after registration).

persona.description: Name of the persona that answered (Oblak or another DeCC0).

suggestions.description: Follow-up questions to offer the visitor.

sources.description: Library document titles the answer drew on.

fallback.description: True when the answer was built from exhibition context alone (Library unreachable).

mode.description: `fast` = direct hybrid reply (or a library-routed ack); `cortex` = Cortex-primary reply. Absent on a context-only fallback.

consulting.description: True when this answer is a brief holding line and the real, Library-sourced answer is being looked up — fetch it from GET /v1/guide/followup. Show a 'consulting the library' state until it arrives.

audioUrl.description: First spoken chunk (mp3 at GET /v1/guide/tts/:id.mp3). Present when TTS is configured and `speak` is not false. Kept for simple clients; prefer `audioUrls`.

audioUrls.description: The whole answer as short, sentence-aligned mp3 chunk URLs — play back-to-back so the voice covers the full message (no mid-message cutoff).

RoomSlot.description: One artwork anchor in the room's GLB, in room-local coordinates (the GLB's own units, identity transform). The quaternion is facing-resolved: local +Z points at the viewer and +Y is re-levelled to world up, so clients can hang a work on it verbatim — no normal flipping or up-vector guessing needed.

id.description: Stable slot id — `Slot_NNN` for slots that exist as GLB nodes, `Auto_NNN` for generated anchors on models without placeholder nodes (un_MUSEUMs).

source.description: `authored` = extracted from a Slot_NNN placeholder node in the GLB; `generated` = deterministically surface-sampled from the onchain slot amount.

quaternion.description: Orientation `[x, y, z, w]`, room-local. Local +Z = resolved facing direction, +Y = upright.

facing.description: Resolved facing direction (unit vector, room-local) — equal to the quaternion's +Z, included for convenience.

flipped.description: True when the placeholder's authored normal pointed into the wall and was flipped during facing resolution.

ambiguous.description: True when the geometry probe found both sides of the slot equally open/closed and the room-interior tie-break decided the facing.

RoomSlotData.description: Baked slot anchors for one room, computed offline from the room's builder GLB (`model_optimized` if present, else `model`) by probing the geometry around each slot to resolve which side of the wall it faces.

model.description: Directus file id of the GLB the slots were computed from. Compare against the room's current model file before trusting the anchors.
